subject: LU-17110 llite: fix slab corruption with fm_extent_count=0

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Etienne, thanks for the patch.

It would be good if you could add a test case that is calling FIEMAP on a bunch of files (e.g. as part of some existing test that already creates a bucnh of files, runtests or similar).

I'd hoped it might be as simple as calling "filefrag" on the files without any arguments (just to get the fragment count), but it looks like filefrag always passes a buffer and sets .fm_extents_count.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

It's better to have some test cases to verify the changes.

Patch Set 1:

> I'd hoped it might be as simple as calling "filefrag" on the files
 > without any arguments (just to get the fragment count), but it
 > looks like filefrag always passes a buffer and sets
 > .fm_extents_count.

No, filefrag is not exposed. It always use extent buffer (fm_extents_count > 0, buf[2048]).

I will think of something to implement a regression test.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Agree, having a test is a good idea and indispensable! It would have been better to have one in LU-16480... 
Honestly, we have suffered a bit to debug the slab memory corruption, it was a painful stuff (but interesting...)

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

Not able to see slab corruption on a true run (running 2.15.3++ +LU-16480+LU-17013+LU-17110):
- 96 mpi ranks 
- 500000 files
- 100000 files in batch

Feels good :)

Patch Set 1:

Gael, did you accidentally remove your Code-Review +1 marker with your last comment?

Patch Set 1:

I agree that having a regression test case is always preferable.

On the flip side, it doesn't help anyone to delay landing this patch which fixes a real issue that causes crashes/corruption and has tested successfully (including manual testing on your system under load).

So while I'd _prefer_ to have a regression test I am still marking it +1 and rerunning the failed tests so it can move forward with landing.  A regression test case could always be added in a later patch.  If the change was more complex and/or had uncertain benefits (i.e. adding a feature, rewriting complex code), then I would be more likely to mark it -1 until a specific test case was included.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I agree that having a regression test case is always preferable.
> 
> On the flip side, it doesn't help anyone to delay landing this patch which fixes a real issue that causes crashes/corruption and has tested successfully (including manual testing on your system under load).
> 
> So while I'd _prefer_ to have a regression test I am still marking it +1 and rerunning the failed tests so it can move forward with landing.  A regression test case could always be added in a later patch.  If the change was more complex and/or had uncertain benefits (i.e. adding a feature, rewriting complex code), then I would be more likely to mark it -1 until a specific test case was included.

Andreas, agree on the methodology. My remark was not for this patch (here we have a clear reproducer and we (generally) try to give some feedback numbers to show the effect with or without the fix) but in LU-16480 there is no reproducer and no nonreg test. I am wondering how we can improve that when patch involves standard ioctl and perhaps lead to memory corruption... Perhaps we can discuss a bit more in the next couple of weeks :)

Patch Set 3:

> Thank you for adding the test case.  Did you check if it triggers the bug without this patch?

Hi Andreas,

The tests (mostly sanity-hsm) was not failing directly in the test. The corruption existed but the consequences were random (this could hang an another thread or crash in another test etc ...).

Now, with several instances of fiemap in parallel, the crash occurs each times. The instances allocate fiemap structures in kmalloc-32. So they get corrupted easily.

parents.subject: LU-17095 build: avoid modules.order nonexistence failure

commit.subject: LU-17110 llite: fix slab corruption with fm_extent_count=0
commit.message:
LU-17110 llite: fix slab corruption with fm_extent_count=0

If userspace uses fiemap with .fm_extent_count=0, .fm_extents[0] is
not allocated. Writing on the first entry without checking the extent
count could lead to memory corruption (slab).

This patch fix also the case when osc is disable: FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST
should be set on the extent (fe_flags) and not on the fiemap struct.

Add a regression test sanityn 71d to test fiemap with
fm_extent_count=0.
Add a regression test sanity-hsm 408 to test fiemap on release files.

Fixes: 4097196 ("LU-11848 lov: FIEMAP support for PFL and FLR file")
Test-Parameters:testlist=sanityn
Test-Parameters:testlist=sanityn env=ONLY=71d,ONLY_REPEAT=20
Test-Parameters:testlist=sanity-hsm env=ONLY=408,ONLY_REPEAT=20
Signed-off-by: Etienne AUJAMES <etienne.aujames@cea.fr>
Change-Id: Id63c6973540187e678020977f2d555dfcbf3c634
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/52352
